records.ety: [Corrupted fr. F. caporal, It. caporale, fr. capo head, chief, L. caput. See Chief, and cf. Caporal.]

defs: A noncommissioned officer, next below a sergeant. In the United States army he is the lowest noncommissioned officer in a company of infantry. He places and relieves sentinels.

records.ety: [L. corporalis, fr. corpus body. See Corpse.]
usage: Bodily is opposed to mental; as, bodily affections. Corporeal refers to the whole physical structure or nature, of the body; as, corporeal substance or frame. Corporal, as now used, refers more to punishment or some infliction; as, corporal punishment. To speak of corporeal punishment is an error. Bodily austerities; the corporeal mold.

defs: Belonging or relating to the body; bodily.
Having a body or substance; not spiritual; material. In this sense now usually written corporeal.
quotes: A corporal heaven . . . .where the stare are.
What seemed corporal melted As breath into the wind.
